steps.title: Compare the shifted values
steps.content: The first five values in data set B are exactly $1$ greater than the first five values in data set A. If $h=27$, then every value in B is exactly $1$ greater than the corresponding value in A, so the standard deviations are equal.

steps.title: Check the possible last values
steps.content: Standard deviation measures spread. If $h=22$, the last value of B is closer to the center than $27$, so B is less spread out. If $h=28$, the last value of B is farther out than $27$, so B is more spread out.

steps.title: Select the only valid statement
steps.content: Only statement I makes data set A have a greater standard deviation than data set B. The answer is A.
